zh.lifeSummary: 當Alexander Alcock於1540年出生在英國英格蘭奧可福爾班波瑞時，他的父親Thomas Alcock41歲，母親Mary Anne Jarvis40歲。他於1549年與Elizabeth Gilman在英國英格蘭結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1605年在他的家鄉享年65歲。

nameMeanings.name: Alcock
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Alcok, a pet form of any of the many medieval personal names beginning with Al-, especially Alan and Alexander. The Middle English hypocoristic suffix -cok (see Cocke ) was very commonly added to personal names in Middle English: compare for example Hancock and Wilcock .

variants.name: Cocke
meanings: nickname from Middle English cok (Old English and Old French coc) ‘rooster’.
patronymic from the Old French and Middle English personal name Cok, of uncertain origin but perhaps derived from the word in 1 above.

variants.name: Alcott
meanings: English: ostensibly a topographic name from any of a large number of places called in Old English as ‘the old cottage’ (Old English (e)ald + cot(e)). In fact, however, it is generally an altered form of Alcock , in part at least for euphemistic reasons.

History: Louisa May Alcott (1832–88), author of Little Women (1869), was the daughter of Amos Bronson Alcott (1799–1888), who had changed the surname from Alcox. The family trace their descent from an Alcocke family who emigrated from England to MA with John Winthrop (see Winthrop ) in 1630.

nameMeanings.name: Alexander
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the Latin form of the Greek name Alexandros, from alexein ‘to defend’ + anēr ‘man, warrior’ (genitive andros). The name became extremely popular in the post-classical period, and was borne by several individuals in the New Testament and some early Christian saints. Its use as a common given name throughout Europe, however, derives largely from the fame of Alexander the Great , King of Macedon ( 356–323 bc ), around whom a large body of popular legend grew up in late antiquity, much of which came to be embodied in the medieval ‘Alexander romances’.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

en.lifeSummary: When Alexander Alcock was born in 1540, in Banbury, Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Thomas Alcock, was 41 and his mother, Mary Anne Jarvis, was 40. He married Elizabeth Gilman in 1549, in England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. He died in 1605, in his hometown, at the age of 65.

ko.lifeSummary: Alexander Alcock(이)가 1540년 영국 잉글랜드 스퍼드실르 반버이에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas Alcock(은)는 41세였고, 그의 어머니 Mary Anne Jarvis(은)는 40세였다. 그는 1549년 영국 잉글랜드에서 Elizabeth Gilman(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1605년 그의 고향에서 65세의 나이로 사망했다.
